case_title: ANNE W THAYER VS. CENTRAL CALIFORNIA BANK et al

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION TO GENERAL AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS FILED BY PLAINTIFF THAYER, ANNE W ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-03-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION/NEW TRIAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CENTRAL CALIFORNIA BANK BY AND THROUGH ITS SUCCESSOR UMPQUA BANK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE 2 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS CCP SECTION 339 UNLESS PLAINTIFF IS CAPABLE OF PLEADING THE EXISTENCE OF A WRITTEN AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE CRLA IS NOT APPLICABLE TO REAL PROPERTY TRANSACTIONS. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED ONLY A PRIVATE DISPUTE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED A FALSE ADVERTISING CLAIM. IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS LEAVE TO AMEND, PLAINTIFF MUST APPEAR AND STATE WHAT IT IS PLAINTIFF INTENDS TO ALLEGE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION/NEW TRIAL FILED BY PLAINTIFF THAYER, ANNE W ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANNE THAYER MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND REVOCATION OF THE COURT'S ORDER DATED JULY 15, 2009. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION DENIED AS UNTIMELY AND PROCEDURALLY INFIRM. COURT LACKS JURISDICTION UNDER APRI INS. CO. VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1999) 76 CAL.APP.4TH 176 AND CCP SECTION 581D. NO BASIS FOR MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL UNDER CCP SECTION 657. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING OF IRREGULARITY IN THE PROCEEDINGS, ACCIDENT OR SURPRISE, NEWLY DISCOVERED EVIDENCE, OR INSUFFICIENCY OF THE EVIDENCE. COMMISSIONER BORICK'S ORDER AND ACTION IRRELEVANT TO THIS LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT. COMMISSIONER BORICK'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WAS ISSUED PURSUANT TO THE TRIAL COURT DELAY REDUCTION ACT TO MOVE THE CASE ALONG AND DID NOT MODIFY THE LAW AND MOTION ORDER. PLAINTIFF APPEARED AT EX PARTE HEARING AND ARGUED THE MERITS THEREBY WAIVING ANY DEFECTS OR IRREGULARITIES IN THE NOTICE. (CARLTON VS. QUINT (2000) 77 CAL.APP.4TH 690.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Relief From Dismissal
Off Calendar Feb-18-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ANNE W. THAYER'S Motion For Relief From Dismissal.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Off Calendar, plaintiff withdrew motion on February 9, 2010. (302/AJR) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR# 8620

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-11-2009 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ANNE THAYER Motion for Reconsideration and revocation of the court's order dated July 15, 2009. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for reconsideration denied as untimely and procedurally infirm. Court lacks jurisdiction under APRI Ins. Co. vs. Superior Court (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 176 and CCP section 581d. No basis for motion for new trial under CCP section 657. Insufficient showing of irregularity in the proceedings, accident or surprise, newly discovered evidence, or insufficiency of the evidence. Commissioner Borick's order and action irrelevant to this law and motion department. Commissioner Borick's Order to Show Cause was issued pursuant to the Trial Court Delay Reduction Act to move the case along and did not modify the law and motion order. Plaintiff appeared at ex parte hearing and argued the merits thereby waiving any defects or irregularities in the notice. (Carlton vs. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Denae Hildebrand Budde, Esq. of Alborg, Veiluva & Epstein 925-939-9880, appearing for defendant / moving party Central California Bank by and through its successor Umpqua Bank
Ernest M. Thayer, Esq. (415436-9495), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Anna W. Thayer on behalf of herself and all others similarly situated

Ruling:
DEFENDANT CENTRAL CALIFORNIA BANK by and through its successor Umpqua Bank's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED with leave to amend as TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE 2 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS CCP SECTION 339 UNLESS PLAINTIFF IS CAPABLE OF PLEADING THE EXISTENCE OF A WRITTEN AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED without leave to amend AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE CRLA IS NOT APPLICABLE TO REAL PROPERTY TRANSACTIONS. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED with leave to amend AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED ONLY A PRIVATE DISPUTE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED without leave to amend AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED A FALSE ADVERTISING CLAIM. IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS LEASE TO AMEND, PLAINTIFF MUST APPEAR AND STATE WHAT IT IS PLAINTIFF INTENDS TO ALLEGE. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 =(302/PJM/JW)
